title: Dendrobium myosurus
intro: Dendrobium myosurus, also known as "deer's ear," is a species of orchid native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its unique, ear-shaped flowers. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Dendrobium myosurus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Dendrobium myosurus is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are used to treat respiratory issues, fever, and other conditions.
taxonomy: Dendrobium myosurus is classified under the family Orchidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as orchids. This genus is further divided into several subgroups based on morphological characteristics and DNA analysis. Dendrobium myosurus specifically belongs to the subgenus Pedagogum. It is native to New Guinea and surrounding islands. The classification of Dendrobium myosurus is subject to ongoing research and possible revisions.
